ksa_text: Knowledge of international data transfer mechanisms under applicable regulations, including adequacy decisions, Standard Contractual Clauses, Binding Corporate Rules, Transfer Impact Assessments, and supplementary safeguards requirements.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Regulatory Compliance KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.42) to justify inclusion for the GenAI CoE Leader. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world GenAI CoE Leader roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Regulatory Compliance is not a standard competency expectation for GenAI CoE Leader. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the GenAI CoE Leader competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.42) and edge-domain placement (Regulatory Compliance) do not meet inclusion threshold for the GenAI CoE Leader. All passes support exclusion.
